entries.title: Gerald Ford
entries.subtitle: Republican
entries.description: Took office after Richard Nixon's resignation and later pardoned him.

entries.title: Jimmy Carter
entries.subtitle: Democratic
entries.description: Brokered the Camp David Accords and presided over the Iran hostage crisis.

entries.title: Ronald Reagan
entries.subtitle: Republican
entries.description: Pursued tax cuts and a defense buildup, and presided over the end of the Cold War.

entries.title: George H. W. Bush
entries.subtitle: Republican
entries.description: Led the Gulf War coalition and oversaw the end of the Soviet Union.

entries.title: Bill Clinton
entries.subtitle: Democratic
entries.description: Presided over a period of economic expansion and was impeached, though not removed, in 1998.

entries.title: George W. Bush
entries.subtitle: Republican
entries.description: Led the response to the September 11 attacks and the wars in Afghanistan and Iraq.

entries.title: Barack Obama
entries.subtitle: Democratic
entries.description: The first Black US president; oversaw the recovery from the 2008 financial crisis and the Affordable Care Act.

entries.title: Donald Trump
entries.subtitle: Republican
entries.description: First term marked by trade disputes with China and the start of the COVID-19 pandemic.

entries.title: Joe Biden
entries.subtitle: Democratic
entries.description: Oversaw the withdrawal from Afghanistan and major infrastructure and climate legislation.

entries.title: Donald Trump
entries.subtitle: Republican
entries.description: Became the second president, after Grover Cleveland, to serve two non-consecutive terms.
